edition.dagbeeld: De Amerikaanse oorlog tegen Iran escaleert: een derde nacht luchtaanvallen, een hernieuwd Hormuz-blokkade en stijgende olieprijzen domineren de editie. Tegelijk versterkt Europa zijn veiligheidspositie met een gezamenlijk raketafweerproject, terwijl thuis de dood van Ann Widdecombe een terrorisme-onderzoek uitlokt en vragen over politieke veiligheid opwerpt.

article.title: Trump’s Cease-Fire Effectively Collapses as He Vows to Restart Blockade and Tolls
article.summary: After several days of strikes, the president notified Congress that fighting had begun again and announced shipping fees that his administration previously deemed illegal.

opening.note: Ceasefire voorbij: Trump herstart blokkade en legt scheepstol op in Hormuz.
opening.label: breaking

article.title: Russia's FSB blamed for Poland grid attack as UK and EU impose first joint cyber sanctions
article.summary: The allies blamed Center 16, the FSB’s signals intelligence arm, for acts of attempted cyber sabotage targeting Poland’s energy sector and water treatment facilities, alongside “a wide range of malicious cyber activities with growing severity.”

article.title: Inside Israel’s Secret Operation to Cultivate Ahmadinejad
article.summary: The yearslong effort to groom the former Iranian president as an intelligence asset culminated in a dramatic effort to take him to an Israeli safe house in the early days of the war. But the plan fell apart.

front.note: NYT onthult hoe Israël jaren probeerde Ahmadinejad als agent te werven.

items.summary: Ali Lmrabet, a Moroccan journalist and critic of the kingdom's government who has been living for many years in Spain, was arrested on charges of spreading false information upon his return to his native country this weekend, his wife said Monday.

items.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.
